name: Cheddar Bread Ring
recipeIngredient: 1 package Active Dry Yeast OR 1 tablespoon Active Dry Yeast (Bulk)
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: You can use Unbleached All-Purpose flour in this recipe and up to 3 cups total.
++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
1. In a large bowl, combine the bread flour, sugar, undissolved yeast, and salt.
2. Heat the milk and butter together until very warm (115-125°F).
3. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the warm liquid and beat at medium speed on an electric mixer for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
4. Add half of the flour and cheese mixture. Beat briefly on high speed on the mixer, scraping the bowl occasionally.
5. Stir in enough additional flour to make a stiff but light dough.
6. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, 5 to 8 minutes.
7. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning once to grease the top. Cover with a dishtowel that has been soaked in hot water and then wrung out until almost dry.
8. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
9. Punch down the dough and turn it out onto a lightly floured surface. Shape into a 20-inch rope.
10. Place seam side down in a buttered 6-7/8 cup ring mold, pinching the ends together. Cover and let rise in warm place until nearly doubled in bulk, about 35 to 40 minutes.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 25 to 30 minutes.
12. Remove from the ring mold. For a softer crust, brush with melted butter while still hot. The crust will become crisp when cool if not brushed.
